repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers/v1 Theorem 2 finite response-path sharpness; pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope/v1 Theorem 1 finite sharp sign-endpoint polytope

published_prior_art.cite: MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018: Bernstein LP MTE/PRTE bounds; MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024: exact-PM multi-IV MTE
MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024: exact-PM multi-IV MTE, no bounded PM-violation sensitivity
JiangSun2023 tests PM; SunWuthrich2025 gives VSIV for pairwise valid instruments, no PRTE support-price zero-sensitivity diagnostic

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Exact-PM limit
soundness_flags.one_line: The eta=0 reduction does not follow as written: omega=0 and nu=0 do not force pi to put zero mass on defier response maps because the displayed constraint is only 0 <= omega <= C_pi pi, so violating pi-mass can be hidden at zero budget.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: K_viol(P) is not well-defined as a function of P alone: the 'homogeneous envelope after removing baseline nonviolating response masses' does not specify which exact-PM primal pi or active face supplies the compensating mass, yet the derivative formula depends on that tangent set.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the violation-budget block with an explicit equality tying violating response-cell mass to pi, or split pi into monotone and violating response-map components so eta=0 algebraically eliminates all defier maps.
Redefine K_viol as the contingent/tangent perturbation set at the exact-PM optimal primal face, including simplex compensation and any dependence on active baseline response-type distributions.
Add a worked m=2, K=1 nondegenerate example that computes the exact-PM face, the first violating-cell perturbations, and the endpoint support prices.

recommended_next_step: Redraft the LP variables and perturbation set before another novelty pass; the novelty kernel survives, but the current zero-budget algebra is load-bearing and false as stated.
